messages.content:
A model for XeO3F2 is shown in the chem3D window. XeO3F2 has trigonal bipyramidal geometry.


ball & stick
-+labels
Rotate the molecule until you have a feeling for its three-dimensional shape. Ignoring the color of the atoms, are all five positions about the central atom equivalent, or are two of them different from the other three? 

If you take the three oxygen atoms, what shape do they define? 

Does the Xe atom lie in the plane defined by the three O atoms? 

Are the F atoms on the same side or on opposite sides of the plane defined by the O atoms? 
opposite

What is the approximate numerical value of the O-Xe-O bond angles? 
90
 degrees.

What is the approximate numerical value of the O-Xe-F bond angles? 
180
 degrees.

The three O atoms are in a set of positons called equatorial. What is the geometry defined by the Xe atom and the three O atoms? 

The two F atoms are in a set of postions called axial. What is the geometry defined by the Xe atom and the two F atoms? 

For practice, type in the name of the geometry of the molecule: fill in the blank 9
square planar 
For practice, type in the name of the set of positions occupied by the three oxygen atoms: fill in the blank 10
equatorial
For practice, type in the name of the set of positions occupied by the two fluorine atoms: fill in the blank 11
axial

 degrees.

For practice, type in the name of the geometry of the molecule: fill in the blank 9

For practice, type in the name of the set of positions occupied by the three oxygen atoms: fill in the blank 10

For practice, type in the name of the set of positions occupied by the two fluorine atoms:

For practice, type in the name of the geometry of the molecule: fill in the blank 9

trigonal bipyramidal

For practice, type in the name of the set of positions occupied by the three oxygen atoms: fill in the blank 10

equatorial

For practice, type in the name of the set of positions occupied by the two fluorine atoms: 

axial
The two F atoms are in a set of postions called axial. What is the geometry defined by the Xe atom and the two F atoms?
The geometry defined by the Xe atom and the two F atoms is linear.
The three O atoms are in a set of positons called equatorial. What is the geometry defined by the Xe atom and the three O atoms?
The geometry defined by the Xe atom and the three O atoms is trigonal planar.
Does the Xe atom lie in the plane defined by the three O atoms?
Yes, the Xe atom lies in the plane defined by the three O atoms.
If you take the three oxygen atoms, what shape do they define?
The three oxygen atoms define a flat triangular shape when considering their positions in the XeO3F2 molecule.

Rotate the molecule until you have a feeling for its three-dimensional shape. Ignoring the color of the atoms, are all five positions about the central atom equivalent, or are two of them different from the other three?

By rotating the molecule, we can observe that the five positions around the central Xe atom in XeO3F2 are not equivalent. Two of them, occupied by the fluorine atoms, are located above and below the plane defined by the three oxygen atoms, while the other three positions, occupied by the oxygen atoms, are in the same plane. Thus, the two positions occupied by the fluorine atoms are different from the other three positions.
What is the approximate numerical value of the O-Xe-O bond angles?
The approximate numerical value of the O-Xe-O bond angles in XeO3F2 is around 120 degrees.
What is the approximate numerical value of the O-Xe-F bond angles?
The approximate numerical value of the O-Xe-F bond angles in XeO3F2 is around 90 degrees.

For each bond, show the direction of polarity by selecting the correct partial charges.


 C-N 



 C-F 



 F-N 



The most polar bond is 
Submit Answer

To determine the direction of polarity for each bond, we need more information or the electronegativity values of the atoms involved. Without this information, it is not possible to accurately determine the partial charges associated with each bond.

As for the question regarding the most polar bond, without the required information, it cannot be determined.
